{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks for the most common type of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma in the orbit. From the retrieved documents, Document [1] explicitly states that 'non-Hodgkin lymphomas, specifically the mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) subtype of low-grade B-cell lymphoma, are the most common orbital lymphomas.' This directly identifies B-cell lymphoma as the most common type in the orbit. Other documents confirm that B-cell lymphomas, including diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) and follicular lymphoma, are prevalent in orbital malignancies. While T-cell, NK-cell, and plasma cell lymphomas are mentioned, they are described as rare subtypes. For example, extranodal NK/T-cell lymphoma is noted as rare and associated with Epstein-Barr virus, and plasma cell lymphomas are not highlighted as common in the orbit. Therefore, based on the evidence, B-cell lymphoma is the most common type.", "answer_choice": "A"}
